_instructions: This is the JSON format Claude produces at the end of every audit session. Save as [client]-audit-data-[month]-[year].json in the project root. Then run: node scripts/fill-template.mjs [filename].json

scores._note: Count of each badge across ALL checks — standard 28 + all B-checks. Must add up to totalChecks.

execSummary.headline: One strong sentence summarizing the audit — e.g. 'Acme Plumbing has strong fundamentals but several technical gaps are actively suppressing search visibility.'
execSummary.body: Two to four sentences. What was strong, what needs work, what the client will have after the remedy.

deliverables._note: One entry per standalone code block included in the report. Empty array if none.
deliverables.desc: LocalBusiness schema JSON-LD — ready to paste into your site's code injection field
llms.txt file — complete content ready to publish at /llms.txt

priorityItems._note: All Criticals first, then High Values. No required count — use however many the findings warrant.
priorityItems.text: Plain-English title of this priority item, as it will appear in the Priority Action List.

remedyItems._note: Must be in the same order as priorityItems. One entry per priority item. When a remedy item covers multiple checks (i.e., two or more audit checks share the same remedyItem number), fill-template.mjs automatically renders a 'This remedy addresses N audit findings' disclosure note below the item title — no extra field needed. Write the findings text to describe all covered checks.
remedyItems.title: Full remedy item title (can be slightly longer than the priority list entry)
remedyItems.findings: One to three sentences describing exactly what was found on this client's site — specific and factual. No remedy steps here.
steps: Step 1: Exact platform path and action, written so a non-technical person can follow it.
Step 3: Add or remove steps — no required count.

remedyItems.title: Second remedy item title
remedyItems.findings: What was found for this check.

remedyItems._note: Example with a standalone code deliverable. Set standalone to null if not applicable.
remedyItems.title: Add LocalBusiness Schema
remedyItems.findings: No structured data was found on the homepage. Google cannot confirm your business name, address, or phone number from machine-readable markup.
steps: Step 1: In Squarespace, go to Settings → Advanced → Code Injection.
Step 2: Paste the JSON-LD block below into the Header field.
Step 3: Click Save, then test at search.google.com/test/rich-results.

standalone.label: Standalone Deliverable — LocalBusiness Schema JSON-LD
standalone.code: <script type="application/ld+json"> { "@context": "https://schema.org", "@type": "LocalBusiness", "name": "Acme Plumbing" } </script>

auditChecks._note: All 28 standard checks required. Keys: C01_1 through C08_2. badgeClass: badge-critical | badge-high | badge-pass | badge-nice. badgeLabel: Critical | High Value | Pass | Nice to Have. remedyItem: integer (1-based) if this check is covered in Remedy Package, null otherwise. ⚠ RULE: Every check with badge-critical or badge-high MUST have a non-null remedyItem. Two checks may share the same remedyItem number only when their fix steps are literally identical. A null remedyItem on a Critical or High Value check is a production error.

C01_1.found: The page title tag is missing entirely.
C01_1.impact: Google has no text to display in search results for this page. It will either show the URL or auto-generate a title, usually poorly.

C01_2.found: Meta description is present but is 210 characters — Google truncates at ~155.
C01_2.impact: The search result snippet gets cut off mid-sentence, which reduces click-through rate.

C01_3.found: Canonical tag present and pointing to the correct URL.
C01_3.impact: Google knows which URL to index. No duplicate content risk.

C01_4.found: Robots meta tag set to index, follow — correct.
C01_4.impact: Search engines can crawl and index this page normally.

C01_5.found: Two H1 tags found on the page.
C01_5.impact: Google expects one H1 per page. Multiple H1s dilute the primary topic signal.

C01_6.found: Inner pages do not have canonical tags.
C01_6.impact: Low risk for a site this size, but worth adding as the site grows.

C02_1.found: OG image present at a reasonable size.
C02_1.impact: Social shares will show a proper preview image.

C02_2.found: og:title, og:description, og:url, og:type all present.
C02_2.impact: Social shares render correctly with correct title and description.

C02_3.found: Twitter card meta tags are absent.
C02_3.impact: X/Twitter will fall back to OG tags, which usually works. Not urgent.

C03_1.found: TTFB measured at 320ms via PageSpeed Insights.
C03_1.impact: Within the acceptable range. No action needed.

C03_2.found: Total load time 2.1s on desktop, 3.4s on mobile.
C03_2.impact: Acceptable. Mobile is slightly slow but not critical.

C03_3.found: Hero image is 2.4MB and served as PNG. No next-gen format in use.
C03_3.impact: This single image is adding ~1.8s to mobile load time.

C03_4.found: Only Squarespace's own scripts loading. No heavy third-party services detected.

C04_1.found: No structured data markup of any kind found on the homepage.
C04_1.impact: Google cannot confirm your business name, address, phone number, or hours from machine-readable data. No eligibility for rich results.

C04_2.found: N/A — no structured data present to evaluate connectivity.
C04_2.impact: Will be resolved when schema is added.

C04_3.found: LocalBusiness, BreadcrumbList, and FAQPage rich result types are all eligible but absent.
C04_3.impact: Missing out on enhanced search result features that competitors may already be using.

C05_1.found: GA4 property G-XXXXXXXXXX detected and firing correctly.
C05_1.impact: Analytics data is being collected. No action needed.

C05_2.found: GA4 tag fires once per page load.
C05_2.impact: No duplicate counting. Data is accurate.

C05_3.found: GA4, Squarespace analytics, and one font loader detected. No unexpected scripts.
C05_3.impact: Clean third-party inventory. No hidden performance or privacy exposure.

C06_1.found: robots.txt exists and allows all crawlers including GPTBot and ClaudeBot.
C06_1.impact: No accidental crawl blocks. AI search crawlers can index the site.

C06_2.impact: Secure connection. No browser warnings for visitors.

C06_3.found: Three external links found without rel="noopener" attribute.
C06_3.impact: Minor security exposure. Easy one-time fix.

C06_4.found: No llms.txt file found at acmeplumbing.com/llms.txt.
C06_4.impact: AI assistants (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Siri) have no structured guidance on how to represent this business. They may describe it inaccurately or incompletely.

C07_1.found: 7 of 12 images on the homepage are missing alt text.
C07_1.impact: Screen readers skip these images entirely. Google cannot index their content.

C07_2.impact: Screen readers and translation tools identify the language correctly.

C08_1.found: Squarespace — Squarespace handles hosting, CDN, and most technical SEO defaults automatically. Platform-specific instructions are provided throughout the Remedy Package.

C08_2.found: No llms.txt, no FAQ schema, no structured NAP data. AI search engines have minimal structured content to work from.
C08_2.impact: When users ask ChatGPT or Perplexity about plumbers in your area, this site is less likely to be cited accurately.

bChecks._note: One entry per additional diagnostic found during the live session. id follows B01, B02 convention. Empty array if no B-checks.
bChecks.name: Mobile Viewport Meta Tag
bChecks.found: Viewport meta tag present and correctly configured.
bChecks.impact: Mobile browsers render the page at the correct scale.

bChecks.name: HTTP to HTTPS Redirect
bChecks.impact: No insecure entry points. Redirect chain is clean.
